confirmed.title: TenantSwitcher tenant-name fallback hardcoded
confirmed.detail: labelFor() returns `Tenant ${tenant.id}` as a raw English literal when a tenant has no name. This string is doubly user-facing: it is rendered as the visible trigger button text (line 61), as each visible dropdown option (line 76), and it is interpolated into the localized aria-label on line 57 (t('arqel.aria.tenant_switch', ...)). The component already imports useArqelTranslations and uses t() for the aria-label, so the English 'Tenant N' fallback is an inconsistent i18n gap — a pt_BR user with an unnamed tenant sees/hears 'Tenant 5' instead of a localized label. Fix: t('arqel.tenant.unnamed', 'Tenant :id', { id: tenant.id }). No such key exists.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at packages-js/ui/src/shell/TenantSwitcher.tsx:47, which reads exactly `tenant.name?.trim() ? tenant.name : ` + "`Tenant ${tenant.id}`" + `. This `Tenant N` fallback is a hardcoded English literal and is triply user-facing: it is the value of currentLabel rendered as the visible trigger button text (line 61), rendered as each visible dropdown option (line 76), and interpolated into the aria-label (line 57). So a pt_BR user with an unnamed tenant sees and hears \"Tenant 5\".

The string IS genuinely localizable yet unlocalized: the component already imports useArqelTranslations (line 13) and binds t (line 42), and already routes the aria-label through t('arqel.aria.tenant_switch', ...) (line 57). The labelFor() fallback bypasses that same t() that is in scope two lines away — a clear inconsistency. No 'arqel.tenant.unnamed' (or any tenant.unnamed) key exists anywhere in packages/ or packages-js/ (grep returned nothing), so a new key with an :id placeholder is needed, exactly as the finding states. The translate util (packages-js/react/src/utils/translate.ts) exists to support this. Not developer-only, not a data-attr, not a console/test string, not already wired.

NOT a duplicate of round 1: the ledger's only TenantSwitcher entry (line 570-581) flagged the aria-label English TEMPLATE ('Switch tenant', 'current:') at line 55, and round 1 fixed it — that is precisely why the file now imports useArqelTranslations and wraps the aria-label in t() at line 57. The labelFor() name fallback is a distinct string that round 1 never addressed; it survived the round-1 fix and is still raw English. The line:concern (47, labelFor fallback) does not match the ledger's entry (55, aria-label template). Genuine remaining defect.

confirmed.title: DashboardFilters date-range 'from' input aria-label hardcoded
confirmed.detail: aria-label={`${filter.label ?? filter.name} from`} on the start-date input is a hardcoded English literal ('from' word) announced to screen readers. The DateRangeControl sub-component does not call useArqelTranslations even though the sibling SelectControl in the SAME file does (line 148 uses t('table.filters.all','All')). So in a pt_BR panel the date-range filter's accessible name speaks English 'from'. The :label portion is data-driven, but the 'from' suffix is raw. Fix: t('table.filters.date_from', ':label from', { label }). No such key exists.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at packages-js/ui/src/widgets/DashboardFilters.tsx:181. The start-date input's accessible name is aria-label={`${filter.label ?? filter.name} from`}, where the suffix "from" is a raw English literal (and line 189 mirrors it with "to"). An aria-label is announced verbatim by screen readers, so in a pt_BR panel the date-range filter speaks English "from"/"to" — genuinely user-facing, not a dev/console/data-attr/test string.

It is genuinely localizable yet unwired: DateRangeControl (lines 159-197) never calls useArqelTranslations and has no t in scope. The hook IS imported at line 11 and is instantiated inside the sibling SelectControl at line 110 (used at line 148: t('table.filters.all','All')) within this very file, proving the i18n plumbing exists in this exact file/package and DateRangeControl simply bypasses it. The auditor's claim matches the code precisely.

Not a duplicate of round 1: the only round-1 ledger entry for this file (confirmed[], ~line 260-268) concerns DashboardFilters.tsx:146 `<option value="">All</option>`, a different string that has since been wired (now t('table.filters.all','All') at line 148). No ledger entry references line 181, DateRangeControl, the from/to aria-labels, or the chrome-hardcoded date-range concern. The suggested key table.filters.date_from does not exist in en/pt_BR table.php (only all, clear, and a legend key under filters), consistent with the finding that a new key is needed.

Real, reproducible, non-duplicate a11y/i18n defect.

confirmed.title: DashboardFilters date-range 'to' input aria-label hardcoded
confirmed.detail: aria-label={`${filter.label ?? filter.name} to`} on the end-date input — same defect as the 'from' input one line set above. The trailing English word 'to' is a raw literal in a screen-reader-announced aria-label, with no t() wiring in DateRangeControl. Fix: t('table.filters.date_to', ':label to', { label }). No such key exists.
confirmed.why_real:
CONFIRMED real, user-facing a11y-i18n defect, not a round-1 duplicate.

Reproduces at packages-js/ui/src/widgets/DashboardFilters.tsx:189: `aria-label={`${filter.label ?? filter.name} to`}` on the end-date input. The trailing word "to" is a raw English literal in a screen-reader-announced aria-label. Its sibling at line 181 has the identical defect ("...from"). Both are inside DateRangeControl (lines 159-197), whose function signature (lines 159-167) receives only {filter, value, onChange} and never gets or calls t — so neither aria-label is wired to translations.

Genuinely user-facing: aria-label is announced verbatim by assistive tech, so a pt_BR panel speaks English "<label> to" / "<label> from" instead of Portuguese. Not a console/test/data-attr string.

Genuinely localizable yet unlocalized: the same file already imports useArqelTranslations (line 11) and binds t (line 110) inside the SIBLING SelectControl component — proving the i18n plumbing is present in this very file and DateRangeControl simply failed to thread t through. The suggested key table.filters.date_to does not exist in packages/core/resources/lang (grep for date_to/date_from returned nothing), so a new key is needed, exactly as the finding states.

NOT a round-1 duplicate: the round-1 ledger contains exactly ONE DashboardFilters.tsx entry — line 146, "Dashboard select filter 'All' option hardcoded" (a visible <option>All</option> literal, a different concern on a different line). No ledger entry (confirmed or refuted) targets the date-range aria-labels at lines 181/189. So this finding was not covered by round 1.

Caveat (does not refute): the finding is the second half of a two-line pair — line 181 ('from') has the identical defect. A proper fix wires both via t('table.filters.date_from'/'date_to'). But the line 189 claim itself is accurate and reproduces.

confirmed.title: FormSection collapse toggle Show/Hide text hardcoded
confirmed.detail: The collapsible-section toggle button renders `{open ? 'Hide' : 'Show'}` — two raw English literals that are visible button text on every collapsible form section. FormSection does not import useArqelTranslations at all, unlike its sibling form components (FormActions uses t('form.save'/'form.cancel'); nativeFields/FieldRenderer use t()). A pt_BR form shows English 'Hide'/'Show'. Needs two new keys, e.g. t('form.section.hide','Hide') / t('form.section.show','Show').
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages-js/ui/src/form/FormSection.tsx:37, which renders `{open ? 'Hide' : 'Show'}` as the visible text of the collapsible-section toggle button (rendered whenever config.collapsible === true). These are two raw English literals shown to end users on every collapsible form section, not a dev-only or sr-only-irrelevant string (and even sr-only labels are treated as in-scope by this ledger). FormSection.tsx does NOT import useArqelTranslations at all, confirmed by grep — whereas its sibling FormActions.tsx (line 9/33) imports and uses `const t = useArqelTranslations()` for t('form.save')/t('form.cancel'). So a pt_BR form renders English Hide/Show. The fix is straightforward: add the hook and two keys (e.g. t('form.section.hide','Hide') / t('form.section.show','Show')). Ledger check: the round-1 .confirmed[] list contains zero FormSection entries (grep -i 'FormSection' returns nothing); covered form-package files are nativeFields.tsx, plus action/table/widget files — none touch this file or this concern. Therefore it is a real defect and not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: Show-page title fallback 'Record' hardcoded
confirmed.detail: PageHeader title={props.recordTitle ?? props.resource?.label ?? 'Record'} — the final fallback 'Record' is a raw English literal rendered as the visible H1 page title when neither a record title nor a resource label is supplied. ArqelShowPage imports no translation hook. While recordTitle/resource.label normally come from the (PHP-translatable) server payload, the hardcoded English default is the only non-localizable link in the chain. Fix: wrap via useArqelTranslations, t('arqel.pages.record','Record').
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at packages-js/ui/src/pages/ArqelShowPage.tsx:46 (the finding's line 47 is one off — line 46 is the title prop): `title={props.recordTitle ?? props.resource?.label ?? 'Record'}`. The final fallback 'Record' is a raw English literal passed to PageHeader, which renders it verbatim as the visible H1 (`

{title}

` in utility/PageHeader.tsx:22). It is genuinely user-facing chrome, not a console/test/data-attr/developer-only string. It is genuinely not localizable: ArqelShowPage.tsx imports no translation hook (only FieldSchema/FormSchema/RecordType types, usePage, FormRenderer, PageHeader). Grep over packages-js/ui/src/pages/ shows zero real t()/useArqelTranslations usages (the `t(` hits are setTimeout/clearTimeout). Meanwhile the @arqel-dev/ui package has an established, pervasively used useArqelTranslations/t(key, fallback) mechanism (DataTable-adjacent siblings, TablePagination, FormActions, ConfirmDialog, etc.). So the string both should and trivially could be wired via t('arqel.pages.record', 'Record'), exactly matching the pattern of the round-1 confirmed fallback-literal findings (table 'Loading…', 'No records found.', etc., which were all confirmed despite only rendering when a prop is absent). Not a duplicate: searched reports/i18n-round1-detection.json for ArqelShowPage / pages/ / recordTitle / pages.record / 'Record' — zero matches in any confirmed or refuted entry. The two nearest ledger entries concern PHP-side Resource::getLabel (Resource.php, refuted) and Field/Column label serialization — different files and different concern (PHP label serialization vs. a frontend React hardcoded H1 fallback). The fallback chain's upstream links (recordTitle/resource.label) come from the PHP payload, but the 'Record' default is the one link with no localization path, which is precisely what the finding claims. Low severity (only shown when both server-provided values are missing), but a genuine, reproducible missing-i18n defect that is not in the round-1 ledger.

confirmed.title: Edit-page title 'Edit {label}' built from hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: PageHeader title={props.recordTitle ?? `Edit ${props.resource?.label ?? 'record'}`} — the visible H1 is hand-built from the raw English verb 'Edit ' plus a 'record' English fallback. ArqelEditPage imports no translation hook, so when recordTitle is absent a pt_BR user sees 'Edit Usuário' / 'Edit record' instead of a localized 'Editar :label'. The resource.label is data-driven but the surrounding 'Edit '/'record' words are not. Fix: t('arqel.pages.edit','Edit :label', { label }).
confirmed.why_real: Reproduces exactly at packages-js/ui/src/pages/ArqelEditPage.tsx:55: title={props.recordTitle ?? `Edit ${props.resource?.label ?? 'record'}`}. The visible H1 (PageHeader title) is hand-built from the raw English verb 'Edit ' plus a 'record' English fallback. Confirmed via Read that ArqelEditPage imports NO translation hook (only useArqelForm, types, @inertiajs/react, and local form/utility components — no useArqelTranslations / t / __). resource.label is data-driven, but the surrounding 'Edit '/'record' literals are not. When the server-supplied recordTitle is absent (the ?? branch is reachable by design), a pt_BR user sees mixed-language 'Edit Usuário' or fully-English 'Edit record' instead of a localized 'Editar :label'. This is genuinely user-facing chrome (page H1), not a dev-only string, not a data-attr, and not already wired through t(). Not a duplicate: grep of the round-1 ledger (53 confirmed + 16 refuted) for ArqelEditPage, recordTitle, recordSubtitle, 'Edit ', pages.edit, ArqelCreatePage, pages/Arqel, and PageHeader returned zero matches — round 1 never covered this file or concern. Caveat noted: recordTitle is the primary path and may often be server-populated, making this the degraded fallback; but the fallback is real, reachable, and emits non-localized text, consistent with round-1's confirmed aria-label-fallback pattern.

confirmed.title: Create-page title 'Create {label}' built from hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: PageHeader title={`Create ${props.resource?.label ?? 'record'}`} — the visible H1 page title is hand-built from the raw English verb 'Create ' plus a 'record' English fallback, with no translation hook imported. A pt_BR panel shows 'Create Usuário' instead of 'Criar :label'. Mirrors the ArqelEditPage gap. Fix: t('arqel.pages.create','Create :label', { label }).
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages-js/ui/src/pages/ArqelCreatePage.tsx:54 — `<PageHeader title={`Create ${props.resource?.label ?? 'record'}`} ...>`. This is the visible H1 page title (rendered chrome, not a console/test/data-attr string), hand-built from the raw English verb 'Create ' plus an English 'record' fallback. The component imports no translation hook (confirmed: no useArqelTranslations/t()/__ in the file), so a pt_BR panel renders 'Create Usuário' instead of a localized 'Criar :label'. The localization path is real and idiomatic in this package: useArqelTranslations is used by 10+ sibling components (FormActions, TableToolbar, ActionMenu, TablePagination, etc.), so the string is genuinely localizable yet unlocalized. The 'mirrors ArqelEditPage' claim is also accurate — ArqelEditPage.tsx:55 uses the identical untranslated `Edit ${...label ?? 'record'}` pattern. NOT a duplicate: a grep of reports/i18n-round1-detection.json for ArqelCreatePage/ArqelEditPage, the pages/ directory, 'Create :label', and the create/record concern returns zero matches; round 1 covered DataTable, TableFilters, TablePagination, TableToolbar, nativeFields, ConfirmDialog, ActionFormModal, ActionMenu, ResourceIndex, widgets, and PHP Field/Column labels — none touch the create/edit page titles.

confirmed.title: Required-field marker is an aria-hidden '*' with no localized accessible text; form.required key exists but unused
confirmed.detail: The only required-field indicator is a visual asterisk: * (lines 53-56). Because it is aria-hidden and the
confirmed.why_real: Reproduces exactly. FieldRenderer.tsx:53-56 renders the required indicator solely as an aria-hidden asterisk: *, and the

confirmed.title: Default CRUD validation path ignores custom messages() and attributes() — :attribute renders as raw snake_case field name, custom messages dropped
confirmed.detail: ResourceController::validated() (line 742) builds rules via extractRules() (which calls only FieldRulesExtractor::extract()) and then calls $request->validate($rules) at line 763 with RULES ONLY — it never passes the second/third args (messages, customAttributes). FieldRulesExtractor::extractMessages() and ::extractAttributes() are never invoked anywhere in this controller (grep confirms only ::extract is used). Consequently, on the framework's default Inertia CRUD save path (the common case; generated FormRequests are opt-in), Laravel's validator falls back to the raw field key for the :attribute placeholder, so a pt_BR user sees e.g. 'O campo user_email é obrigatório' / 'The user_email field is required' with the un-humanized, un-localized snake_case name, and any per-field validationMessage() the app set is silently ignored. The translated validation.php lines DO resolve (locale infra works), but the :attribute substitution is wrong because the human/localized field label is never supplied. Fix: $request->validate($rules, $extractor->extractMessages($fields), $extractor->extractAttributes($fields)).
confirmed.why_real:
VERIFIED REAL, NOT A DUPLICATE.

Mechanics confirmed in code:
- ResourceController::validated() (packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:742) builds rules via extractRules() and calls $request->validate($rules) at line 763 with RULES ONLY — no second (messages) or third (customAttributes) argument.
- extractRules() (line 939) instantiates Arqel\Form\FieldRulesExtractor and invokes ONLY ->extract() (line 957). It never calls extractMessages() or extractAttributes().
- The extractor DOES provide both: FieldRulesExtractor::extractMessages() (packages/form/src/FieldRulesExtractor.php:56) aggregates per-field {name}.{rule} => message in the exact shape Laravel's validate() messages arg consumes, and extractAttributes() (line 80) maps field name => getValidationAttribute(). Both are dead on the default CRUD save path. grep confirms only ::extract is used in the controller (the other ::validate at line 400 is the index/query path, same omission).

Consequence: on the framework's default Inertia save path (generated FormRequests are opt-in), any per-field validationMessage() and validationAttribute() the app configured is silently dropped during server-side validation. This is a genuine, user-facing functional defect (it affects the validation error text users see) and it reproduces.

Severity caveat (one claim overstated, does not refute): the finding says the user sees raw snake_case "user_email". In reality, when no validationAttribute is set Laravel falls back to its native :attribute resolution which humanizes "user_email" -> "user email" (and consults validation.attributes lang keys if present). So the raw-snake_case rendering is inaccurate for the no-config default. But the core defect stands: the field's configured/localizable attribute label is never supplied, and explicitly-set custom messages/attributes are dropped — that is the real bug regardless of the humanization nuance.

Not a duplicate of the round-1 ledger:
- The only adjacent entry is the REFUTED finding at packages/fields/src/Concerns/HasValidation.php:197 ("validationAttribute serialized raw"). That concerned whether the SERIALIZER should auto-wrap app-supplied strings in __() — refuted as correct Laravel convention. It is a different layer/concern and even presupposes the extractor is the intended carrier; it says nothing about the controller failing to wire extractMessages()/extractAttributes() into validate(). No ledger entry covers this controller wiring gap. Searched the full confirmed[] and refuted[] sets for ResourceController / extractMessages / extractAttributes / validate() / :attribute — no match for this file:line/concern.

Not dev-only, not already-wired, not a false positive. Fix is as stated: $request->validate($rules, $extractor->extractMessages($fields), $extractor->extractAttributes($fields)).

confirmed.title: Native inputs lack aria-invalid when a field has errors — no localized invalid-state association
confirmed.detail: Native inputs set aria-describedby (which does include the error element id, good) but never set aria-invalid={true} when errors are present (lines 154-155 and the parallel inputs at 177-179, 202-203, 261-262, 386-387, etc. all wire aria-describedby/required but no aria-invalid). FieldRenderer computes hasError but does not pass an invalid flag to the input. Without aria-invalid, assistive tech does not announce the control as being in an error state in the user's locale (the browser's own localized 'invalid'/'required entry' semantics never fire for the error case). This is an a11y-i18n gap adjacent to, but distinct from, the Round-1 chrome findings.
confirmed.why_real:
Reproduces at code level. FieldRenderer.tsx computes `hasError = errors && errors.length > 0` and wires the error element id into `describedBy`, but passes only `inputId`/`describedBy` to the input — never an invalid flag. NativeProps (nativeFields.tsx:29-38) has no `invalid` member, and every native control (TextLike:146-157, Textarea:170-182, NumericInput:194-214, Checkbox:226-235, SelectInput:256-272, MultiSelectInput:286, RadioGroup:316, BelongsToInput:351, DateInput:379, FileInput:401) sets aria-describedby/required but never `aria-invalid`. The intent to support it is evident: the shadcn primitives (input.tsx:13, textarea.tsx:10, select.tsx:40, checkbox.tsx:15, button.tsx, badge.tsx) all carry `aria-invalid:` styling hooks that never fire for the native fallback path. So screen readers do not announce these controls as being in an error state — a genuine, user-facing a11y defect.

Not a duplicate: the round-1 ledger touches nativeFields.tsx only for hardcoded chrome strings (line 109 unregistered-field notice, line 350 BelongsTo placeholder) and contains no aria-invalid / invalid-state entry; grep for "aria-invalid"/"invalid" in the ledger returns only the unrelated validation-lang-file finding.

Caveat on framing: the "i18n" label is a stretch. The error message text itself is rendered (errors[0]) and associated via aria-describedby, and it is server-supplied/localizable. The missing attribute is a pure boolean ARIA state with no localizable string; the "localized invalid-state semantics" angle is secondary. This is fundamentally an accessibility defect rather than an internationalization one — the auditor itself admits it is "distinct from the Round-1 chrome findings." But the underlying defect is real, reproduces, and is not already fixed.

confirmed.title: DateCell date mode uses toLocaleDateString() with no locale and ignores the server format/timezone props
confirmed.suggested_key: n/a (pass active locale + honor props.format/timezone to Intl.DateTimeFormat)
confirmed.detail: DateCell renders `date.toLocaleDateString()` with no locale argument, so the date is formatted using the *browser/runtime* default locale (commonly en-US 'MM/DD/YYYY') rather than the active Arqel panel locale. The component imports no translation/locale source (grep confirms cells.tsx has zero locale/Intl/useArqelTranslations references). Worse, this is also a plumbing bug: DateColumn.php (packages/table/src/Columns/DateColumn.php:25-32) ships a `format` prop (default 'Y-m-d') and the type DateColumnProps (packages-js/types/src/tables.ts:74-75) declares both `format?` and `timezone?`, but DateCell never reads `column.props.format` or `column.props.timezone`. So a developer calling ->date('d/m/Y') gets the prop serialized and silently dropped; output depends solely on the viewer's OS locale. A pt_BR user on an en-US machine sees American-ordered dates; the configured format is dead.
confirmed.why_real: CONFIRMED real, not a duplicate. Code reproduces at packages-js/ui/src/table/cells.tsx: DateCell's date mode (line 155) renders date.toLocaleDateString() with no locale argument, so dates are formatted in the browser/runtime default locale (commonly en-US MM/DD/YYYY) rather than the active Arqel panel locale. Same locale-less pattern at line 153 (datetime mode, toLocaleString()). The plumbing bug is also confirmed: DateColumn.php:25-32/53-59 serializes a `format` prop (default 'Y-m-d') via getTypeSpecificProps(), and DateColumnProps in packages-js/types/src/tables.ts:74-75 declares both `format?` and `timezone?`, but DateCell never reads column.props.format or column.props.timezone — both are silently dropped. So a developer calling ->date('d/m/Y') gets the prop serialized and ignored; output depends solely on the viewer's OS locale. This is genuinely user-facing (rendered table cell text) and unwired (cells.tsx imports no Intl/locale/translation source). NOT a duplicate: the round-1 ledger (53 confirmed + 16 refuted) has zero entries for cells.tsx, DateCell, toLocaleDateString, locale-format, timezone, or the date format-prop gap; round 1 covered only DataTable/TableFilters/TablePagination/TableToolbar/form chrome aria-labels and PHP label/option serialization. The only nuance: NumberCell (lines 169-187) DOES honor its separator props, so the finding correctly scopes the defect to date/datetime rendering — the date claim holds.

confirmed.title: DateCell datetime mode uses toLocaleString() with no locale argument
confirmed.detail: For mode==='datetime', DateCell renders `date.toLocaleString()` with no locale and no options. The displayed date+time ordering, separators, 12h/24h clock, and AM/PM are taken from the runtime default locale (en-US gives 'M/D/YYYY, h:mm:ss AM') instead of the panel's active locale. The `format` prop from DateColumn.php is again ignored. Locale-insensitive: identical hardcoded-en-US-assumption defect as the date branch, on the datetime branch.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages-js/ui/src/table/cells.tsx:153. In DateCell, the `mode==='datetime'` branch renders `<time dateTime={date.toISOString()}>{date.toLocaleString()}</time>`. `Date.prototype.toLocaleString()` is called with no `locales` and no `options` argument, so the rendered date+time string (component ordering, separators, 12h/24h clock, AM/PM) is governed by the JS runtime's default locale (commonly en-US -> 'M/D/YYYY, h:mm:ss AM'), not the panel's active locale. This is genuinely user-facing visible text in a data table, not a dev-only/aria string. The PHP side (packages/table/src/Columns/DateColumn.php:35-57) accepts a `dateTime(format='Y-m-d H:i:s')` and serializes a `format` prop, which this branch ignores entirely — confirming the auditor's 'format prop ignored' claim. cells.tsx imports no translation/locale hook, so no active-locale plumbing reaches this call. NOT a duplicate: the round-1 ledger (53 confirmed entries) contains zero references to cells.tsx, DateCell, toLocaleString, or any dates-numbers/locale-format concern; its dimensions are exclusively chrome-hardcoded, field-labels-php, aria-a11y-i18n, and i18n-plumbing. Confidence high on reproduction and non-duplication. The finding is the datetime sibling of the same defect family as the date branch (line 155), but it is a distinct code path with distinct output and is correctly scoped to line 153.

confirmed.title: formatRelative emits hardcoded English relative-time strings ('Xs/m/h/d ago')
confirmed.detail: DateColumn ->since() (mode 'since') routes to formatRelative(date), which builds relative times by string concatenation: `${seconds}s ago`, `${minutes}m ago`, `${hours}h ago`, `${days}d ago` (lines 160-166). The ' ago' suffix and the s/m/h/d unit abbreviations are hardcoded English literals with no localization and no use of Intl.RelativeTimeFormat. A pt_BR user sees '3d ago' instead of 'há 3 dias'. Not in the Round 1 ledger (which covered only aria/chrome text). Sibling package versioning/VersionTimeline.tsx already uses Intl.RelativeTimeFormat (though hardcoded to 'en' — separate finding), so the relative-time concept is locale-capable elsewhere but this table cell is not.
confirmed.why_real: Reproduces verbatim at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/ui/src/table/cells.tsx:158-167. formatRelative() builds relative-time strings by concatenation: `${seconds}s ago`, `${minutes}m ago`, `${hours}h ago`, `${days}d ago`. The ' ago' suffix and s/m/h/d unit abbreviations are hardcoded English literals with no localization and no Intl.RelativeTimeFormat. DateCell routes to it when column.props.mode === 'since' (lines 149-150), rendering the output as visible `<time>` cell text. So a pt_BR user genuinely sees '3d ago' instead of 'há 3 dias' — a real user-facing locale-format defect, not a dev-only/console/data-attr string and not already wired through t(). NOT a duplicate: scanned the round-1 ledger (.confirmed[] and refuted) for cells.tsx, formatRelative, 'since', 'relative', 'locale-format', 'dates-numbers', and Intl — zero matches. Round 1 covered only aria/chrome text literals in DataTable, TableFilters, TablePagination, TableToolbar, nativeFields, ConfirmDialog, CommandPalette, and login-form; the date/relative-time formatting concern in cells.tsx was never assessed. Severity is modest (the 'since' mode is opt-in per column) but the claim is accurate and reproduces.

confirmed.title: NumberCell defaults to en-US grouping ('.' decimal / no thousands) and drops the money() currency prop entirely
confirmed.detail: NumberCell formats via num.toFixed() + manual regex grouping using column.props.thousandsSeparator and column.props.decimalSeparator (default '.'). When the app does not set separators (the common path), output is raw en-US-shaped ('1234.50', no grouping). More importantly this is a documented-but-unwired serialization bug: NumberColumn.php money($currency) (packages/table/src/Columns/NumberColumn.php:27-32) emits a `currency` prop and its docblock claims 'the React side picks the right Intl.NumberFormat', but NumberColumnProps (packages-js/types/src/tables.ts:78-84) has NO `currency` field and NumberCell never reads it. So ->money('EUR') produces no currency symbol, no locale grouping, no Intl.NumberFormat — the currency is silently discarded. Number formatting is never locale-aware; it depends solely on whether the developer manually passed separators.
confirmed.why_real:
CONFIRMED, not a duplicate.

NOT A DUPLICATE: Grep of the round-1 ledger (/home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/reports/i18n-round1-detection.json) for numbercell, numbercolumn, currency, money, thousand, decimalSeparator, dates-numbers, number format, and cells.tsx returns ZERO matches. The ledger's dates-numbers-adjacent entries are all about aria labels, sr-only text, and locale-negotiation in PHP — none touch NumberCell or NumberColumn. The file packages-js/ui/src/table/cells.tsx is not flagged anywhere in confirmed[] or refuted[].

REPRODUCES (load-bearing claim — dropped currency):
- packages/table/src/Columns/NumberColumn.php:27-32 money($currency) stores $this->currency, and getTypeSpecificProps() (line 61) emits 'currency' => $this->currency into the serialized column payload. The class docblock (lines 12-13) explicitly claims "the React side picks the right Intl.NumberFormat."
- packages-js/types/src/tables.ts:78-84 NumberColumnProps has NO currency field (only decimals, thousandsSeparator, decimalSeparator, prefix, suffix). NumberColumnSchema (line 114) = ColumnBase<'number', NumberColumnProps>.
- packages-js/ui/src/table/cells.tsx:169-187 NumberCell never references column.props.currency; it only reads decimals, thousandsSeparator, decimalSeparator, prefix, suffix. So ->money('EUR') ships a currency prop that is silently discarded: no symbol, no Intl.NumberFormat, no locale grouping. Confirmed the PHP-emits/TS-omits/React-ignores chain end to end; this is the documented-but-unwired serialization family (the most persistent bug class per project history).

SECONDARY claim (en-US-shaped default grouping, never locale-aware) is also literally true: line 173-178 uses num.toFixed() + manual regex grouping that only groups when thousandsSeparator is explicitly set, decimalSeparator defaults to '.', and there is no Intl.NumberFormat anywhere — so output is en-US-shaped on the common path. This is the weaker/systemic part, but it does not undermine the verdict.

USER-FACING: NumberCell renders visible table cell content, not a console/test/data-attr string. Genuine, reproducing, non-duplicate defect.

confirmed.title: VersionTimeline.formatRelativeTime hardcodes the 'en' locale in Intl.RelativeTimeFormat
confirmed.detail: The module-level RTF constant is `new Intl.RelativeTimeFormat('en', { numeric: 'auto' })` — the locale is the hardcoded literal 'en'. formatRelativeTime() (used to render every version's '2 hours ago' timestamp in the timeline) therefore always produces English relative times regardless of the active Arqel locale. A pt_BR panel shows 'in 2 days'/'3 hours ago' instead of 'em 2 dias'/'há 3 horas'. The Intl machinery is already in use; only the locale argument is wrong. Should derive from the active panel locale (useArqelTranslations locale / i18n.locale prop / navigator.language fallback) rather than a literal.
confirmed.why_real: CONFIRMED, not a duplicate. packages-js/versioning/src/VersionTimeline.tsx:36 reads exactly `new Intl.RelativeTimeFormat('en', { numeric: 'auto' })` — a module-level constant with the locale hardcoded as the literal 'en'. This RTF is the only formatter used by formatRelativeTime() (line 64/67), which is called at line 148 (`const relative = formatRelativeTime(version.created_at)`) to render every version's relative timestamp in the visible, exported VersionTimeline feed. The output is therefore genuinely user-facing: a pt_BR panel shows English 'in 2 days' / '3 hours ago' instead of 'em 2 dias' / 'há 3 horas'. It is not dev-only, not a data-attr/console string, and not already wired — there is no locale derivation anywhere; the constant is locked to 'en'. The monorepo HAS the machinery to fix it: @arqel-dev/i18n exposes useTranslation()/useI18nContext() carrying the active `locale`, which could feed Intl.RelativeTimeFormat(locale,...), so the literal is genuinely localizable and the omission is the defect. Ledger check: grep for VersionTimeline/formatRelativeTime/RelativeTimeFormat/locale-format/dates-numbers/versioning across reports/i18n-round1-detection.json returns nothing; round 1's confirmed[] and refuted[] cover only packages-js/ui table/form/action chrome plus a handful of PHP and i18n-core files — this file:line is absent. Minor caveats that do not refute: the component's tests are describe.skip'd (FIXME post-shadcn migration) and a caller could in principle pass a pre-formatted value, but the live exported runtime path renders the hardcoded-English label by default.

confirmed.title: CurrencyField PHP defaults are hardcoded en-US ($ , .) and never derive from app locale
confirmed.suggested_key: n/a (derive separators/symbol from Intl/NumberFormatter on active locale)
confirmed.detail: CurrencyField defaults prefix='$' (line 24), thousandsSeparator=',' (line 28), decimalSeparator='.' (line 30) — explicitly en-US-shaped (the docblock admits 'Defaults are en-US shaped'). These props are serialized to CurrencyInput.tsx, whose formatNumber() applies them verbatim (the docblock there falsely claims reformat 'via Intl.NumberFormat', but it is manual string grouping using these server props). With no Intl/locale anywhere in the chain, any app that does not manually override every separator for every currency field renders '$1,234.50' to a pt_BR user who expects 'R$ 1.234,50'. There is no framework hook tying these defaults to app()->getLocale(); the only escape is per-field manual configuration. Locale-insensitive currency formatting by construction.
confirmed.why_real:
Source-verified and reproduces. CurrencyField.php:24/28/30 hardcode en-US defaults (prefix '$', thousandsSeparator ',', decimalSeparator '.'); the docblock at lines 11-13 itself admits "Defaults are en-US shaped". getTypeSpecificProps() (lines 65-74) serializes these verbatim to the client. CurrencyInput.tsx:33-36 and formatNumber() (lines 71-76) apply them via manual string grouping (`replace(/\B(?=(\d{3})+(?!\d))/g, thousands)`) — confirming the finding's claim that the component docblock's "reformatted via Intl.NumberFormat" (line 5) is FALSE; there is no Intl call and no locale awareness anywhere in the chain. No `app()->getLocale()` derivation exists; the only escape is per-field manual override. A pt_BR user with no override sees "$1,234.50" instead of "R$ 1.234,50". This is a genuine user-facing locale-format (dates-numbers) defect.

Not a duplicate: grepped the entire round1 ledger (.confirmed[] and refuted) for currency/CurrencyField/CurrencyInput/prefix/separator/NumberFormat — the only formatting-adjacent entry is a LocaleSwitcher separator-normalization bug (HandleArqelInertiaRequests.php:422), an entirely different file/concern. Round 1 covered chrome-hardcoded UI labels via the t() pipeline, not numeric/currency formatting.

Caveat lowering confidence to med: the defaults are documented design (docblock points to Field::priceBRL macro) and are overridable via prefix()/thousandsSeparator()/decimalSeparator() setters, so a reviewer could frame this as "by design / app responsibility" rather than a bug. But the finding's narrow claim — locale-insensitive-by-construction with no framework hook to app locale — is accurate and reproduces, and the misleading "via Intl.NumberFormat" docblock makes the gap a real trap. Relevant files: packages/fields/src/Types/CurrencyField.php and packages-js/fields-js/src/number/CurrencyInput.tsx.

confirmed.title: Breadcrumb 'Create' label hardcoded in PHP builder
confirmed.detail: buildFor() emits ['label' => 'Create', 'url' => null] as a raw English literal for the create-action breadcrumb. This array is serialized straight into the Inertia payload and rendered verbatim by the breadcrumb nav, so it never follows app()->getLocale(). The nav package contains ZERO __()/trans()/Lang:: calls (grep confirms none in packages/nav/src). Crucially the translation keys ALREADY EXIST in both locales — packages/core/resources/lang/{en,pt_BR}/arqel.php define 'create' => 'Create'/'Criar' and messages.php likewise — so this is purely an unwired literal, not a missing key. A pt_BR panel shows English 'Create' in the breadcrumb trail. Round 1 only fixed the frontend aria-label='Breadcrumb' (Breadcrumbs.tsx:54), not these server-emitted crumb labels. Framework-hardcoded (not app-must-key): the app cannot override it. Fix: __('arqel::arqel.create') (returns the key gracefully if untranslated, matching the Column getLabel pattern at packages/table/src/Column.php:239).
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed real, non-duplicate user-facing i18n defect at packages/nav/src/BreadcrumbsBuilder.php:78. buildFor() emits ['label' => 'Create', 'url' => null] as a raw English literal that is serialized straight into the Inertia breadcrumb payload and rendered verbatim by the breadcrumb nav. grep confirms the entire packages/nav/src/ directory contains zero __()/trans()/Lang:: calls, so it never consults app()->getLocale(); a pt_BR panel shows English 'Create' in the breadcrumb trail. The sibling 'Edit' literal at line 95 is the same bug, confirming the pattern is real and not a one-off misread. A translation for 'Create' already exists in BOTH locales (packages/core/resources/lang/{en,pt_BR}/arqel.php), so this is an unwired literal, not a missing key. It is framework-hardcoded (the app cannot override it). NOT a duplicate: the only breadcrumb entry in the round-1 ledger is the frontend aria-label='Breadcrumb' landmark at packages-js/ui/src/utility/Breadcrumbs.tsx:54 (key breadcrumb.label) — a different file (TSX vs PHP), different line, and different concern (landmark accessible name vs server-emitted crumb text). Round 1 never touched BreadcrumbsBuilder.php or any server-emitted labels. One minor inaccuracy in the finding: the correct translation key path is arqel::arqel.actions.create (the value lives under the 'actions' namespace, not a top-level 'create'), so the suggested __('arqel::arqel.create') would miss; this is a fix detail and does not negate the defect.

confirmed.title: Breadcrumb 'Edit' label hardcoded in PHP builder
confirmed.detail: buildFor() emits ['label' => 'Edit', 'url' => null] as a raw English literal for the edit-action breadcrumb (sibling to the 'Create' literal at line 78). Same defect class: serialized verbatim into the Inertia breadcrumb payload, no __()/trans() in the entire nav package, and the matching keys already exist in both locales (arqel.php 'edit' => 'Edit'/'Editar', messages.php 'edit'). A pt_BR user sees English 'Edit' in the breadcrumb trail. Framework-hardcoded, not app-keyable. Round 1's Breadcrumbs.tsx:54 fix was only the nav landmark aria-label, never these PHP labels. Fix: __('arqel::arqel.edit').
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed real and not a duplicate. packages/nav/src/BreadcrumbsBuilder.php:95 emits ['label' => 'Edit', 'url' => null] as a raw English literal (sibling to the 'Create' literal at line 78). The class docblock confirms buildFor() returns ['label' => string, 'url' => ?string] arrays serialized into the Inertia breadcrumb payload, so this is genuinely user-facing chrome rendered in the breadcrumb trail, not a dev-only/console/test string. grep confirms the entire nav package has zero __()/trans()/Lang:: calls. Matching translation keys already exist in both locales: en/arqel.php 'edit' => 'Edit', pt_BR/arqel.php 'edit' => 'Editar' (also mirrored in messages.php and actions.php), so a pt_BR user sees English 'Edit' despite a ready Portuguese value. Framework-hardcoded, not app-keyable. Fix: __('arqel::arqel.edit'). NOT a duplicate: the only Breadcrumb entry in the round-1 ledger is packages-js/ui/src/utility/Breadcrumbs.tsx:54 (a React TSX nav-landmark aria-label='Breadcrumb') — different file, language, line, and concern; the PHP BreadcrumbsBuilder.php is absent from the ledger entirely. Minor caveat: the 'Create' literal at line 78 and the ucfirst() resource label at line 67 share the same defect class but were not flagged; this does not undermine the validity of the flagged 'Edit' line.

confirmed.title: Widget heading & description serialized verbatim (never through __())
confirmed.detail: Widget::toArray() emits 'heading' => $this->heading (line 238) and 'description' => $this->description (line 239) raw. These are set by the app via heading()/description() (lines 67/74) and stored verbatim; getHeading()/getDescription() (166/171) return them unwrapped. They are the visible dashboard widget title and subtitle (rendered by WidgetWrapper/WidgetRenderer), genuinely user-facing — not data-attrs. The widgets package has ZERO __()/trans() calls anywhere (grep packages/widgets/src). So even though Action/Column expose a __()-resolving getter pattern (Action.php:385, Column.php:239), Widget never does — a heading('Sales') can never be localized; switching locale leaves the English title. This is the widget-title equivalent of the Field/Column/Action label gap that round 1 fixed, but in the unexamined widgets package. Severity high because dashboard widget titles are prominent chrome. App-must-key in practice (app supplies the string) but the framework provides no translation hook, so even __()-keyed strings aren't resolved by the framework — same shape as the round-1 Action label finding.
confirmed.why_real:
CONFIRMED real, NOT a duplicate.

Reproduces in source: packages/widgets/src/Widget.php toArray() (lines 233-249) emits 'heading' => $this->heading (line 238) and 'description' => $this->description (line 239) completely raw. heading()/description() (lines 67/74) store them verbatim; getHeading()/getDescription() (164/171) return them unwrapped. grep over packages/widgets/src finds ZERO __()/trans() calls. These two fields are the visible dashboard widget title/subtitle consumed by the React WidgetRenderer/WidgetWrapper — genuine user-facing chrome, not data-attrs, not dev-only.

Why it is a genuine framework gap (not merely "app-must-key"): the PHP layer has a deliberate, documented serialization-time localization pattern that Widget alone omits. Action::toArray() routes label/tooltip/notifications through self::localizeAction() → __() (Action.php:354/385). Column::getLabel() routes through self::localizeLabel() → trans() (Column.php:228-251). Field::getLabel() uses the identical trans() pattern (Field.php:181-203). These resolve app-supplied strings against the active-request locale at serialization, so a keyed heading WOULD localize if Widget followed the convention. Widget provides no such hook, so even a __()-keyed heading('users.dashboard.sales') is shipped untranslated and switching locale leaves the English string — exactly the shape of the round-1 Action-label finding. Severity high is reasonable: widget titles are prominent dashboard chrome.

Not a duplicate: round-1 ledger (reports/i18n-round1-detection.json) has 0 entries referencing packages/widgets; the file does not appear in any confirmed/refuted .file field. Covered PHP files were Action.php, Column.php, Field.php, Resource.php, Section.php, Tab.php, Filter.php, SelectField.php, HasValidation.php, SelectColumn.php, SelectFilter.php, TernaryFilter.php, Table.php, LocaleController.php, HandleArqelInertiaRequests.php, TranslationLoader.php — Widget.php is absent. The "heading"/"WidgetWrapper" substring hits in the ledger are incidental prose inside unrelated JS-side findings. The fix is the same localize* wrapper applied to lines 238-239 (or in the getters).

Minor caveat (does not refute): like the round-1 Action label, this is the app that ultimately supplies the heading string, so part of the burden is on app authors to pass keys — but the framework still owes the resolution hook it provides everywhere else, and currently does not, which is the defect.

confirmed.title: Export download abort messages hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: ExportDownloadController::download() (the web route GET /admin/exports/{exportId}/download, routes/admin.php:9) aborts with raw English strings: abort(400, 'Invalid export id.') at line 41, abort(404, 'Export not found.') at line 48, abort(404, 'Export ambiguous.') at line 52. Because this is a browser-facing GET route (not JSON-only), the abort message renders in Laravel's HTML error page and is user-visible. The export package has ZERO __()/trans() calls. Framework-hardcoded; no key exists. A non-English admin who hits an expired/invalid export link sees English error text. Lower severity (error edge cases) but a genuine server-emitted user-facing string gap that round 1 did not touch.
confirmed.why_real:
VERIFIED REAL, NOT A DUPLICATE.

Reproduction (file read confirms verbatim): packages/export/src/Http/Controllers/ExportDownloadController.php contains raw English abort messages at line 41 abort(400, 'Invalid export id.'), line 48 abort(404, 'Export not found.'), line 52 abort(404, 'Export ambiguous.').

User-facing: routes/admin.php (export package) registers Route::middleware(['web','auth'])->get('/admin/exports/{exportId}/download', ...). Because it is a web (HTML) GET route, abort($code, $message) surfaces the message in Laravel's HTML error page, visible to a logged-in admin who hits an invalid/expired/ambiguous export link. Not JSON-only, not dev-only, not console/test/data-attr.

Not localized: grep for __( / trans( / Lang:: / ->translate / @lang across packages/export/src returned zero matches — the entire export package has no i18n wiring, and no key exists for these strings. The framework demonstrably has locale infra (en + pt_BR lang dirs, __('arqel::messages...') in ResourceController), so the strings are localizable in principle but bypass it. A non-English admin sees English error text.

Not a duplicate: searched the round-1 ledger (53 confirmed + 16 refuted) for ExportDownloadController, packages/export, export, abort, 404/400, download, error-page — no entry references the export package or any abort/HTML-error-page i18n concern. Round 1 covered table/form/action/widget UI chrome and PHP field/column/action labels only.

Severity is genuinely low (error edge cases on a narrow path), but it is a legitimate server-emitted user-facing missing-i18n string gap untouched by round 1.

confirmed.title: LocaleController 'Invalid locale.' abort hardcoded
confirmed.detail: LocaleController rejects an unknown locale with abort(422, 'Invalid locale.') — a raw English literal. This is the user-facing rejection message when switching panel locale via the LocaleSwitcher; it surfaces as the 422 response body. Round 1 touched LocaleController:38 (the allowlist normalization plumbing bug) but NOT this abort message text. The core package uses __('arqel::messages.flash.*') elsewhere (ResourceController) so the infra exists and the convention is established; this string simply bypasses it. Framework-hardcoded, no matching key. Low severity (edge-case error) but a real server-emitted untranslated string.
confirmed.why_real:
Reproduces verbatim: /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/LocaleController.php:44 reads exactly `abort(422, 'Invalid locale.')` — a raw English literal, not wired through any translator. It is a server-emitted string surfaced as the 422 response body when switching panel locale to a value outside the allowlist.

Not a duplicate: The two round-1 ledger entries on this file both target line 38 (`in_array(...)` allowlist + availableLocales() normalization/asymmetry — the plumbing logic bug). Neither addresses the abort message text at line 44. Distinct concern; round 1 did not cover it.

Genuinely localizable and bypasses an established convention: The same core package routes user-facing copy through __('arqel::messages.*'). ResourceController uses __('arqel::messages.flash.*') in 6 places, and messages.php already defines an 'errors' namespace (forbidden, not_found) where an 'invalid_locale' key would be idiomatic. This literal simply skips that infra. It is not a dev-only string (not console.log/test/data-attr) — it is the actual HTTP rejection body returned to the client.

Severity is low and honestly noted: it is an error-path edge case (the LocaleSwitcher UI only offers valid options, so it requires a tampered/stale POST to trigger; the body is a raw abort, not rendered into visible chrome). Sibling aborts (MetricsController abort(404)/abort(403)) pass no message, so this is the outlier that injects a hardcoded literal. Real but minor missing-i18n defect, not previously fixed.
